paper_title: Geological Context and Palynological Analysis of Marine Deposits

methods.description: This method involves the analysis of dinoflagellate cysts and acritarch species in sediment samples to determine their preservation, diversity, and biostratigraphic value. It is used to establish a relative dating of the sediment sample based on the occurrence of specific species and their known age ranges.

steps.description: The specimen originates from marine deposits of the Breda Formation, which includes Langhian to Gelasian strata.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from Breda Formation

steps.description: The preservation and diversity of the dinocysts in the sediment sample taken from NMR 9991-12018 are moderate; many specimens are torn, folded, or broken.
steps.searchable_summary: assess preservation and diversity of dinocysts

steps.description: A total of 20 dinoflagellate cyst species and one acritarch species were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: record species of dinoflagellate cysts and acritarchs

steps.description: The biostratigraphical analysis with dinoflagellate cysts and the relative dating of the sediment sample relies on range data from the North Sea Basin and the north Atlantic realm.
steps.searchable_summary: perform biostratigraphical analysis using range data

steps.description: Although reworking of pre-Neogene dinoflagellate cysts species is considerable, several species have a biostratigraphic value.
In summary, a tentative relative age between 7.6 Ma and 5 Ma (late Tortonian-earliest Zanclean) can be proposed for this sediment sample.
steps.searchable_summary: propose relative age for sediment sample
